hawkish_phrases: some modest tightening of policy, at a gradual pace and to a limited extent, may be needed
unit labour costs have continued to grow at rates above those consistent with meeting the inflation target in the medium term
excess demand and domestic inflationary pressures were expected to build gradually
CPI inflation was projected to rise to slightly above the 2% target towards the end of the forecast period
dovish_phrases: If global growth fails to stabilise or if Brexit uncertainties remain entrenched, monetary policy may need to reinforce the expected recovery
Monetary policy could respond in either direction to changes in the economic outlook
business investment and export orders have remained weak
There continue to be some signs that the labour market is loosening
CPI inflation remained at 1.5% in November
summary: The MPC voted 7-2 to hold Bank Rate at 0.75%, with a genuinely two-way risk assessment acknowledging both potential need for easing if risks materialise and possible modest tightening if recovery proceeds as expected.
